presentation.title: AI Workbench Local — Policy / Contract Comparison
presentation.subtitle: Executive comparison review of grounded document differences
presentation.author: AI Workbench Local

executive_summary:
The Access Review Evidence Log and Privileged Account Approval Email reflect distinct but interrelated control mechanisms: one documents evidence gaps in vendor access reviews, while the other governs conditional, time-bound approvals for privileged system access. Both emphasize accountability and compliance, but differ in scope and enforcement triggers.

- The Access Review Evidence Log focuses on validating completed evidence for Q1 2024 vendor controls, with unresolved gaps like EV-002 blocking closure.  
- The Privileged Account Approval Email governs conditional access for specific administrators, requiring ongoing oversight and revalidation upon personnel changes.  
- Evidence gaps in the Access Log are technical and procedural, tied to artifact provenance; approval in the Email hinges on logging compliance and vendor confirmation.  
- Closure of the Access Log depends on full provenance verification, whereas the Email approval requires archival of documentation and quarterly reviews.  
- The Access Log is a retrospective audit artifact; the Email is a proactive, conditional control with lifecycle dependencies.  
- Both rely on documentation archiving but serve different phases: one validates past activity, the other enforces future compliance.
key_highlights: The Access Review Evidence Log and Privileged Account Approval Email reflect distinct but interrelated control mechanisms: one documents evidence gaps in vendor access reviews, while the other governs conditional, time-bound approvals for privileged system access. Both emphasize accountability and compliance, but differ in scope and enforcement triggers.
The Access Review Evidence Log focuses on validating completed evidence for Q1 2024 vendor controls, with unresolved gaps like EV-002 blocking closure.
The Privileged Account Approval Email governs conditional access for specific administrators, requiring ongoing oversight and revalidation upon personnel changes.
Evidence gaps in the Access Log are technical and procedural, tied to artifact provenance; approval in the Email hinges on logging compliance and vendor confirmation.
Closure of the Access Log depends on full provenance verification, whereas the Email approval requires archival of documentation and quarterly reviews.
The Access Log is a retrospective audit artifact; the Email is a proactive, conditional control with lifecycle dependencies.
